key_points: Off-campus landlords routinely change rent after leases are signed
Specific apartments (The Brexx/Retreat) charged $2,000 in bogus damage fees
Dorm fires reported; dryer lint traps cited as hazard at Shulze Hall
key_quotes: They literally changed my rent after I had signed my lease, and there is no way for me to prove it
DO NOT live at The Villas at Happy Valley — they required us to empty our fridge by 8am with no warning
The landlord issue in State College should be studied

key_points: Greek life hazing is a documented, serious problem — not just rumors
Fraternity culture described as toxic; one student regretted joining a 'top' frat
Football game day student section has major crowd management failures
key_quotes: I got yelled at by almost everyone except my pledge brothers and thought that was just how it worked
Got to the gate at 2:30 and didn't get to scan my ticket until 4:00
3 fraternity brothers sentenced to jail in Penn State hazing death

key_points: Creamery (on-campus ice cream) mentioned positively by multiple alumni
No specific widespread dining hall complaints surfaced in data
Off-campus food delivery blocked from most major apartment buildings
key_quotes: Fresh ice cream at the Creamery
We do not have access to your dorms — The Standard, The Maxxen, The Haus, The Rise, either Oliv's

key_points: Branch campus closures causing real distress for working-class students
Students working 35-40 hours weekly while taking 5-6 courses — unsustainable
Isolation and loneliness reported frequently by freshmen and transfer students
key_quotes: I found it hard working 35 hours and doing 5 courses, working 40 hours and 5 to 6 courses sounds miserable
I've spent the past two weekends alone in my dorm and never felt so lonely before
I am miserable here as my only friends come from my fraternity

key_points: Penn State is notoriously stingy with merit scholarships
Out-of-state cost is prohibitively high — students regularly turn PSU down for this reason
Honors College requires massive essay investment with no guaranteed aid boost
key_quotes: I wish penn state didn't require me to go 200k in debt if I went
Receiving $15k from PSU is a huge achievement — they are typically stingy with merit
PSU would be 25k/year with my Schreyers scholarship

key_points: Smeal (accounting/business) and engineering programs are genuinely well-regarded
Honors College is considered one of the best in the country but extremely hard to get into
AI cheating is rampant and disrupting group project integrity
key_quotes: Penn State is ranked like #23 for accounting, probably higher than a couple of the ivies
It is generally regarded as one of the best if not the best honors colleges in the world
There are people in my class who blatantly use AI in discussion posts, online exams, homework

key_points: President received 47% raise while staff got 2.4-2.9% and campuses closed
Board of Trustees unanimously shut down WPSU public radio to cut $3.4M from $10B budget
Canvas LMS got hacked mid-finals week; IT response took over 24 hours
key_quotes: President Bendapudi got a 47% raise... roughly $27,000 per week
I stopped considering donating after they closed the branch I went to for a budget shortfall less than the president's raise
The trustees can UNANIMOUSLY vote against keeping WPSU open but have millions to give the president a huge raise

key_points: University Park is widely praised as a quintessential college town experience
State College is geographically isolated — no car means feeling stuck
Campus traffic and pedestrian safety are recurring complaints
key_quotes: Very good school, fun and safe location. The epitome of a 'college town'
It's very hard to get out of state college/PA if you don't have a car
Stop tailgating people, put your phone down — it's only the first week and there's been plenty of accidents

key_points: Career fairs are a genuine standout — companies actively recruit and value PSU grads
Strong alumni network frequently cited as a real-world advantage
On the West Coast, PSU brand recognition is weak — often confused with UPenn
key_quotes: One of the most amazing things about Penn State is the career fairs — companies seriously value our university
Penn State has an amazing alumni network and opportunities
Almost no one on the west coast even knows UPenn exists — Penn State is just such a big name

key_points: In-state tuition is manageable; out-of-state cost is a dealbreaker for many
Honors College Schreyers scholarship (25k/yr) makes PSU genuinely competitive
Rising tuition and poor merit aid make cost-benefit math difficult for middle-income families
key_quotes: PSU would be 25k/year with my Schreyers scholarship
I wish penn state didn't require me to go 200k in debt — settling for a school with a worse aerospace program
Why donate to PSU in the first place? My tuition is plenty for them

key_points: Massive school spirit and football culture define the social atmosphere
THON raises $18M for children's cancer — genuinely inspiring student-run tradition
Campus pride is real, but institutional scandals create a complicated identity
key_quotes: My time at Penn State was life defining. Best of luck!
THON is incredible — a student organization that raises $18M for children's cancer
I've been a Penn State fan since essentially the day I can remember — born and raised

key_points: Safety: State College Police underreported hundreds of rapes over nearly a decade
Hazing: Three fraternity brothers sentenced to jail in a student hazing death
Scandal: Former professor sentenced after being caught masturbating and performing lewd acts on campus 55+ times
key_quotes: State College Police Department underreported hundreds of rapes in public data, masking the true extent of crime
At Penn State, critics claim, there's no handle on hazing
Former PSU professor Themis Matsoukas sentenced to 5-20 months after seen in videos at least 55 times

key_points: THON is the largest student-run philanthropy in the world — $18M raised annually
The Arboretum on campus is free, beautiful, and gives out pumpkins in fall
Career fairs bring top recruiters who genuinely prioritize Penn State candidates
key_quotes: The largest student-run charitable event in the country — THON
Gorgeous arboretum with free festive pumpkins in the fall
I got my job at the spring career fair at PSU when I was a senior
